# Product Requirements Document
# Generated by req-engine from raw input (Catalog-driven dashboard library, Coker v1, Apr 2026)
# ---

## 1. Project Overview

SPM ONE shall evolve from loosely defined chart widgets to a **catalog-driven dashboard system**. Each **equipment type** (starting with **Coker**) ships with a **versioned knowledge pack**: approved **parameter families**, **presentation templates** (visual and interaction patterns matching client reference screens), and **whitelisted per-template options**. Engineers compose dashboards by placing templates on a grid, setting a **dashboard-level time or cycle context**, optionally adjusting allowed options, and **overriding display titles** on placed blocks. **Only the product team** may change the catalog through **releases**. **Asset-level add-on parameters** may extend the base type when the product team publishes them. Users may submit an in-app **parameter request** that routes to the product team only. **Print and PDF export** shall reproduce the same themed layout as the on-screen dashboard. The first delivery scope is a **Coker v1** pack sufficient to build **six** client reference dashboards (asset information and 3D model, operations and sensor monitoring, fatigue overview, bulging inspection, crack and FAD, per-cycle analysis).

## 2. Goals & Success Criteria

1. A user with edit permission can create a new dashboard for a Coker asset, open the **Widget Library** filtered to **Coker**, and place-only from the **published catalog** (no ad-hoc chart types).
2. A user can set **one primary dashboard context** (time range and/or selected cycle) that applies to all placed templates that are defined to follow dashboard context.
3. A user can **duplicate** a dashboard to another asset of the **same equipment type**; layout, titles, and option values copy; data rebinds to the target asset.
4. A user can **rename** the display title of any placed template instance without changing the underlying parameter binding.
5. A user can submit a **Request new parameter** from the library or editor; the request is stored and visible to the product team role in-app (or equivalent admin queue); the requester receives acknowledgment of submission.
6. **Export to PDF** (or print) of a saved dashboard preserves widget positions, Coker theme styling, and textual labels within a defined tolerance (see NFR-03).
7. The **Coker v1** pack includes **all presentation templates** required to reproduce the **six** reference dashboard layouts described in the Coker template inventory (Section 8 / Assumptions), subject to design sign-off.
8. Every saved dashboard record stores **equipment type pack version** and **per-instance config** (template id, position, size, user title, allowed options) so that older layouts remain loadable after a pack update.

## 3. Target Users

- **Primary user:** Integrity or process engineer — composes and reads equipment dashboards, sets global time/cycle context, exports for reports.
- **Secondary user:** Product team member — receives **parameter requests**, plans catalog changes in releases, publishes **pack** updates.
- **Tertiary user (assumed):** Viewer with share link — read-only full-screen and export.

## 4. Functional Requirements

FR-01: The system shall associate every **Equipment** record with exactly one **equipment type** key (e.g. coker) for catalog resolution.

FR-02: The system shall define an **EquipmentKnowledgePack** as a versioned release artifact that binds one equipment type to a set of **ParameterFamily** rows and **WidgetTemplate** definitions.

FR-03: The system shall store **ParameterFamily** rows with a stable string identifier, display name, category (Asset Information, Asset Efficiency, Event Visualization, Other), and data contract metadata for engineering consumption.

FR-04: The system shall store **WidgetTemplate** rows with a stable string identifier, display name, thumbnail reference, default grid width and height, minimum width and height, and a fixed **kind** (kpi_card, time_series, gauge, area_chart, data_table, heatmap_2d, schematic_3d, polar_plot, bar_chart, fad_chart, unwrapped_map, control_time_range, multi_tab_panel, empty_state, composite_kpi_strip).

FR-05: The system shall link each **WidgetTemplate** to exactly one **ParameterFamily** or to a **control** pseudo-family for global dashboard controls.

FR-06: The system shall persist **option schema** for each **WidgetTemplate** as a whitelisted set of user-editable options (e.g. facing direction, sub-metric) with no free-form style keys.

FR-07: The system shall scope **Widget Library** search and browse by the active dashboard’s **equipment type**; templates for other types do not appear.

FR-08: The system shall show **add-on** parameter families in the library when the target **Equipment** has published add-on flags from the product team.

FR-09: The system shall let the user override only the **display title** of a placed **DashboardWidget** instance; default title comes from the **WidgetTemplate** or **ParameterFamily**.

FR-10: The system shall persist each **WorkspaceDashboard** widget item as: template id, x, y, w, h, minW, minH, user title override, option values, and **pack version** at save time.

FR-11: The system shall provide a **dashboard context bar** on the editor canvas for: primary time range and/or **cycle id** selection, and **latest update** readout when applicable to the pack.

FR-12: The system shall apply **dashboard context** to every placed instance whose template is marked **follows_dashboard_context** in the pack.

FR-13: The system shall allow a template to be marked **ignores_dashboard_context** when the product definition requires a fixed window (e.g. last completed cycle only); the editor shows this behavior in the template description.

FR-14: The system shall let the user set **per-instance** options only where the template’s option schema allows; invalid combinations are blocked at save time.

FR-15: The system shall let the user open a **Request new parameter** form with: free-text description, optional category, optional reference to the current **Equipment**, and submit action.

FR-16: The system shall persist each **ParameterRequest** with: id, requester user id, equipment id, status (submitted, acknowledged, closed), body text, created at, updated at.

FR-17: The system shall make **ParameterRequest** records visible in a **product team** or **admin** surface with read and status transition actions.

FR-18: The system shall not send **ParameterRequest** notifications to arbitrary end users who are not in the product team role (request stays internal to the product workflow).

FR-19: The system shall offer **Duplicate dashboard** for a target **Equipment** of the **same equipment type**; the duplicate copies layout, titles, and options.

FR-20: The system shall rebind data paths on duplicate so that series, tags, and asset-specific bindings resolve to the new **Equipment** id.

FR-21: The system shall prevent duplicate onto an **Equipment** of a different **equipment type**; the UI shows a blocking message.

FR-22: The read-only **dashboard popup** and **full-screen viewer** shall render placed templates using the same **WidgetTemplate** renderers as the editor **preview** mode.

FR-23: The system shall provide **export to PDF** (or system print) from the full-screen viewer or an explicit export action with **no app chrome** on the export.

FR-24: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **equipment data** as a two-column key-value table of static attributes (asset name, dimensions, inspection date, cycle count, total damage, and related fields from the R1 reference).

FR-25: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **3D equipment model** view with segment-color legend, elevational dimension markers, top-down orientation diagram, and axis triad (R1 reference).

FR-26: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **max-value KPI strip** of at least four single-metric cards with vertical accent bars (R2 reference).

FR-27: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **multi-series time series** with Y-axis label, X-axis date, legend with sensor tags, All action, Invert action, and pagination when series exceed one page (R2 reference).

FR-28: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **quenching** data block with an **empty state** when flow rate and steam rate are unavailable (R2 reference).

FR-29: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **time information** with duration control, from date, to date, latest update readout, and a horizontal range slider (R2 reference).

FR-30: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **sensor location** on a vessel schematic with labeled callout points (R2 reference).

FR-31: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **fatigue KPI strip** of four cards: total damage, remaining life, last assessment date, last cycle id (R3 reference).

FR-32: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **sensor data of last cycle** as a multi-line chart with a quantity sub-selector when the pack defines one (R3 reference).

FR-33: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **remaining life** as a semicircular gauge with colored bands and center value (R3 reference).

FR-34: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **accumulated damage** as a filled area series with cycle on the X-axis (R3 reference).

FR-35: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **top N damage locations** data table with damage, azimuth, elevation, direction, and group name columns (R3 reference).

FR-36: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **total fatigue damage** on a vessel with heatmap coloring, facings control, color scale, and numbered hotspot callouts (R3 reference).

FR-37: The Coker v1 pack shall include **WidgetTemplate** entries for **maximum PSLF** and **inspection campaign id** as compact summary cards (R4 reference).

FR-38: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **top N bulging severity** rows with PSLF, likelihood badge, azimuth, elevation, bulging magnitude, and zone columns (R4 reference).

FR-39: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **bulging inspection** as a 2D elevation-versus-azimuth heatmap with mode toggles among bulging, PSLF, and ovality (R4 reference).

FR-40: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **crack details** as a multi-column table of crack locations, cycle, zone, position, length, depth, and assessment ratios (R5 reference).

FR-41: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **FAD curve** (Lr vs Kr) with acceptable region fill, FAD line, and current crack point marker (R5 reference).

FR-42: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **crack specification** as an unwrapped shell map with section labels and selectable crack highlight (R5 reference).

FR-43: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **cycles information** as a wide block with at least four sub-columns: max temperature, max pressure, max stress, max single damage with sub-lines for sensor or location (R6 reference).

FR-44: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **cycle selection** with cycle id, status, and start-end timestamps (R6 reference).

FR-45: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **sensor data of the selected cycle** as a time-series line chart (R6 reference).

FR-46: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **top drum displacement** as a polar plot with lateral and vertical readouts (R6 reference).

FR-47: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for **total single fatigue damage by elevation** as a vertical bar chart (R6 reference).

FR-48: The Coker v1 pack shall include a **WidgetTemplate** for a **tabbed media panel** with at least **Temperature**, **Displacement**, and **Stress** tabs; the panel shall support a documented placeholder for missing image content (R6 reference).

FR-49: The system shall apply a **Coker reference theme** (color tokens, card chrome, chart chrome, table zebra, typography scale) to all **Coker v1** template renderers.

FR-50: The system shall version **Coker** pack releases as semantic version or integer **packVersion**; loading a dashboard saved on an older version shall use forward-compatible default option values for new keys.

FR-51: The system shall either auto-map deprecated **WidgetTemplate** ids to successor templates on load or show a non-destructive **incompatible** notice per product policy.

FR-52: The **Module Library** (drag source) in the **Dashboard** module editor shall list **WidgetTemplate** entries grouped by the four **categories** (Asset Information, Asset Efficiency, Event Visualization, Other).

FR-53: The system shall save new placed widgets with **template id** and **options** object; the read path shall support legacy **viewType**-only records during a transition (see Assumptions).

FR-54: The system shall enforce template-level **min** and **max** size guardrails in the grid so a heatmap or schematic cannot shrink below the pack’s readability minimum.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

NFR-01: Performance — The dashboard editor shall achieve first interactive paint within 3 seconds after navigation on a development-class laptop for a dashboard with at most 20 placed widgets; mock data only in v1.

NFR-02: Accessibility — New template chrome shall meet **WCAG 2.1 AA** for text contrast and focus order on the editor, viewer, and export for standard widgets (heatmaps and complex schematics: best-effort alt and textual summaries where a full text equivalent is infeasible; flag for design).

NFR-03: Fidelity — PDF export for a 1080p viewport shall preserve widget bounding boxes with positional error no greater than 2% of container width relative to the on-screen grid; typography shall use the same design tokens as the Coker theme.

NFR-04: Auditability — All **ParameterRequest** records are retained for 24 months or until product policy deletes them (assumption: client has no DSR delete requirement for internal requests; verify).

NFR-05: Security — **ParameterRequest** data is not exposed via public share links; only authenticated org users and product team views see request content (mock auth in v1).

## 6. Out of Scope

- Client-side or customer-self-service editing of the **ParameterFamily** catalog or **WidgetTemplate** registry.
- Arbitrary Recharts or custom SQL-style exploration like full **Power BI**; users cannot create new chart types or bind arbitrary data sources.
- Real-time collaboration on the same dashboard edit session beyond the existing **single active editor lock** rule.
- Mobile-first layout of the full engineering dashboard (tablet/narrow is best-effort).
- 3D CAD interchange or on-site LiDAR ingestion.
- Direct email integration for **ParameterRequest** (in-app only unless added later).

## 7. Constraints

- The implementation must stay compatible with **Next.js App Router** and the existing **Dashboard module** editor route (`/dashboard/dashboard/[dashboardId]/edit`) as the sole authoring surface.
- The implementation must use **Zustand**-backed **WorkspaceDashboard** persistence for layout until a backend exists; export may use client-side rendering and print.
- **Coker** reference images in the product archive are the **acceptance** baseline for Coker v1 **visual** template QA.
- **Equipment** ids for primary mock assets (`equipment-a` for Coker 01) remain stable.

## 8. Assumptions Made

- [ ] The six reference dashboards map to: **R1** Asset information + 3D model; **R2** Operations (max cards, multi-charts, quenching, time, sensor map); **R3** Fatigue overview (KPI, charts, table, heatmap); **R4** Bulging inspection; **R5** Crack and FAD; **R6** Per-cycle and displacement — verify naming with design.
- [ ] **Equipment** **equipment type** is modeled as a string key on **Equipment** or a join table; exact schema is an implementation choice.
- [ ] **Mock data** for Coker v1 is sufficient for visual QA; real-time plant data is out of scope for functionality beyond binding hooks.
- [ ] **ParameterRequest** “product team” visibility is implemented as a **role** flag on **User** or a **debug** admin route in v1.
- [ ] **Legacy** widget payloads in existing `WorkspaceDashboard.widgets` remain parseable for one transition release via adapter mapping from old `viewType` to new **WidgetTemplate** ids.
- [ ] **PDF** generation uses browser print to PDF or a headless print pipeline; exact library is not mandated by the PRD.

## 9. Open Questions

- [ ] Should **ParameterRequest** allow file attachments (e.g. client spec PDFs) in v1 or text only?
- [ ] Should a **pack** downgrade (user loads dashboard saved with newer pack than client bundle) show read-only with upgrade prompt, or block open?

## 10. Suggested Task Breakdown Hints

- Domain model: Equipment type, **EquipmentKnowledgePack**, **ParameterFamily**, **WidgetTemplate**, **ParameterRequest**, dashboard **widget instance** shape
- Data: Coker v1 **seed** and mock binders for six reference flows
- Theming: **Coker** reference theme tokens and card/chart chrome
- Template renderers: split by **template kind**; engineering visuals (heatmaps, schematics) as dedicated components
- **Widget Library** UI: category tree, search, add-on display, request form
- **Editor** integration: context bar, **template** drag payload, **option** inspector, **title** override, grid constraints
- **Read-only** paths: popup, full viewer, **adapter** from legacy widget shape
- **Export**: print stylesheets and PDF trigger
- **Product team** **ParameterRequest** queue UI
- QA: **per-reference-screen** **acceptance** checklist
